name: exif

metadata.name: exif
metadata.summary: Read and modify image EXIF metadata using Python.

documentation:
# [exif] Package

```{image} https://www.gitlab.com/TNThieding/exif/badges/master/pipeline.svg
:target: https://gitlab.com/TNThieding/exif
```

```{image} https://www.gitlab.com/tnthieding/exif/badges/master/coverage.svg
:target: https://gitlab.com/TNThieding/exif
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/exif/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://exif.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:target: https://github.com/psf/black
```

```{image} http://www.mypy-lang.org/static/mypy_badge.svg
:alt: Checked with mypy
:target: http://mypy-lang.org/
```

Read and modify image EXIF metadata using Python without any third-party software
dependencies. For example, batch process image metadata using a Python script.

:::{note}
I developed this package in 2018 as a hobby; however, I no longer have the same bandwidth
to work on this project. As always, contributions and bug fixes are welcome and appreciated.
If this package does not suit your needs in its current form, I encourage you to investigate
alternative packages such as [piexif], [Pillow], or the like.
:::

## Quick Start

Open an image with EXIF metadata using the Python `open()` built-in function. Ensure the
binary mode flag is set. Pass this image file object into the `exif.Image` class:

```
>>> from exif import Image
>>> with open('grand_canyon.jpg', 'rb') as image_file:
...     my_image = Image(image_file)
...
>>> my_image.has_exif
True
```

List EXIF attributes using the `list_all()` method:

```
>>> my_image.list_all()
['_exif_ifd_pointer', '_gps_ifd_pointer', 'aperture_value', 'brightness_value', 'color_space',
 'components_configuration', 'compression', 'datetime', 'datetime_digitized', 'datetime_original', 'exif_version',
 'exposure_bias_value', 'exposure_mode', 'exposure_program', 'exposure_time', 'f_number', 'flash',
 'flashpix_version', 'focal_length', 'focal_length_in_35mm_film', 'gps_altitude', 'gps_altitude_ref',
 'gps_datestamp', 'gps_dest_bearing', 'gps_dest_bearing_ref', 'gps_horizontal_positioning_error',
 'gps_img_direction', 'gps_img_direction_ref', 'gps_latitude', 'gps_latitude_ref', 'gps_longitude',
 'gps_longitude_ref', 'gps_speed', 'gps_speed_ref', 'gps_timestamp', 'jpeg_interchange_format',
 'jpeg_interchange_format_length', 'lens_make', 'lens_model', 'lens_specification', 'make', 'maker_note',
 'metering_mode', 'model', 'orientation', 'photographic_sensitivity', 'pixel_x_dimension', 'pixel_y_dimension',
 'resolution_unit', 'scene_capture_type', 'scene_type', 'sensing_method', 'shutter_speed_value', 'software',
 'subject_area', 'subsec_time_digitized', 'subsec_time_original', 'white_balance', 'x_resolution',
 'y_and_c_positioning', 'y_resolution']
```

Access EXIF metadata tags using Python attribute notation:

```
>>> # Read tags with Python "get" notation.
>>> my_image.gps_latitude
(36.0, 3.0, 11.08)
>>> my_image.gps_longitude
(112.0, 5.0, 4.18)
>>> my_image.model
'iPhone 7'
>>>
>>> # Modify tags with Python "set" notation.
>>> my_image.make = "Python"
>>>
>>> # Delete tags with Python "del" notation.
>>> del my_image.gps_latitude
>>> del my_image.gps_longitude
>>>
>>> # Add new tags with Python "set" notation.
>>> from exif import LightSource
>>> my_image.light_source = LightSource.DAYLIGHT
```

Write the image with modified EXIF metadata to an image file using `open()` in binary
write mode:

```
>>> with open('modified_image.jpg', 'wb') as new_image_file:
...     new_image_file.write(my_image.get_file())
...
```

Refer to the [usage page](https://exif.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html) for information and examples of alternative ways to access EXIF tags (e.g.
with index/item syntax or with methods).

[piexif]: https://pypi.org/project/piexif/
[pillow]: https://pypi.org/project/Pillow/

functions.name: exif._utils.value_fits_in_ifd_tag
documentation:
Determine if value fits inside the IFD tag itself.

```
param Structure tag_dt

IFD tag datatype instance

returns

value fits inside IFD tag

rtype

bool
```

documentation: Status of the camera's flash when the image was taken. (Reported by the `flash` tag.)

documentation: Utility to create empty APP1 metadata bytes.

functions.name: exif._app1_create.generate_empty_app1_bytes
documentation:
Generate an empty APP1 segment with IFDs 0, EXIF, and GPS.

```
returns

big endian APP1 segment with 3 IFDs

rtype

bytes
```

documentation: EXIF datatype indicator for IFD structure.

classes.name: exif._constants.ExposureMode
documentation: Exposure mode set when the image was shot.

classes.name: exif._constants.ExposureProgram

class_variables.name: exif._constants.ExposureProgram.CREATIVE_PROGRAM
documentation: Creative Program (Biased Toward Depth of Field)

class_variables.name: exif._constants.ExposureProgram.ACTION_PROGRAM
documentation: Action Program (Biased Toward Fast Shutter Speed)

class_variables.name: exif._constants.ExposureProgram.PORTRAIT_MODE
documentation: Portrait Mode (For Closeup Photos with the Background out of Focus)

class_variables.name: exif._constants.ExposureProgram.LANDSCAPE_MODE
documentation: Landscape Kode (For Landscape Photos with the Background in Focus)
Class of the program used by the camera to set exposure when the picture is taken.

classes.name: exif._constants.GpsAltitudeRef
documentation: Altitude used as the reference altitude.

classes.name: exif._constants.LightSource
documentation: Class of the program used by the camera to set exposure when the picture is taken.

classes.name: exif._constants.Orientation

class_variables.name: exif._constants.Orientation.TOP_LEFT
documentation:
The 0th row is at the visual top of the image and the 0th column is the visual
left-hand side.

class_variables.name: exif._constants.Orientation.TOP_RIGHT
documentation:
The 0th row is at the visual top of the image and the 0th column is the visual
right-hand side.

class_variables.name: exif._constants.Orientation.BOTTOM_RIGHT
documentation:
The 0th row is at the visual bottom of the image and the 0th column is the visual
right-hand side.

class_variables.name: exif._constants.Orientation.BOTTOM_LEFT
documentation:
The 0th row is at the visual bottom of the image and the 0th column is the visual
left-hand side.

class_variables.name: exif._constants.Orientation.LEFT_TOP
documentation:
The 0th row is the visual left-hand side of the image and the 0th column is the
visual top.

class_variables.name: exif._constants.Orientation.RIGHT_TOP
documentation:
The 0th row is the visual right-hand side of the image and the 0th column is the
visual bottom.

class_variables.name: exif._constants.Orientation.RIGHT_BOTTOM
documentation:
The 0th row is the visual right-hand side of the image and the 0th column is the
visual bottom.

class_variables.name: exif._constants.Orientation.LEFT_BOTTOM
documentation:
The 0th row is the visual left-hand side of the image and the 0th column is the
visual bottom.
Image orientation in terms of rows and columns.

documentation: Image sensor type on the camera or input device.

documentation: Legacy Windows XP style tag structure parser module.

classes.name: exif.ifd_tag._windows_xp.WindowsXp

methods.name: exif.ifd_tag._windows_xp.WindowsXp.modify
documentation:
Modify tag value.

```
param value

new tag value

type value

corresponding Python type
```

methods.name: exif.ifd_tag._windows_xp.WindowsXp.read
documentation:
Read tag value.

In string types, the count refers to how many characters exist in the string. Windows XP tags are encoded in
UCS2/UTF-16, not ASCII.

```
returns

tag value

rtype

corresponding Python type
```
Legacy Windows XP style tag structure parser class.

modules.name: exif.ifd_tag._byte
documentation: IFD BYTE tag structure parser module.

classes.name: exif.ifd_tag._byte.Byte

methods.name: exif.ifd_tag._byte.Byte.modify
documentation:
Modify tag value.

This method does not contain logic for unpacking multiple values since the EXIF standard (v2.2) does not list
any IFD tags of BYTE type with a count greater than 1.

```
param value

new tag value

type value

corresponding Python type
```

any IFD tags of BYTE type with a count greater than 1.

```

tag value

corresponding Python type

documentation:
Remove the specified attribute from the image.

```
param attribute

image EXIF attribute name
```

methods.name: exif._image.Image.delete_all
documentation: Remove all EXIF tags from the image.

methods.name: exif._image.Image.get
documentation:
Return the value of the specified tag.

If the attribute is not available or set, return the value specified by the `default`
keyword argument.

```
param attribute

image EXIF attribute name

param default

return value if attribute does not exist

returns

tag value if present, default otherwise

rtype

corresponding Python type
```

methods.name: exif._image.Image.get_all
documentation: Return dictionary containing all EXIF tag values keyed by tag name.

methods.name: exif._image.Image.get_file
documentation:
Generate equivalent binary file contents.

:returns: image binary with EXIF metadata

methods.name: exif._image.Image.get_thumbnail
documentation:
Extract thumbnail binary contained in EXIF metadata.

```
returns

thumbnail binary

raises RuntimeError

image does not contain thumbnail
```

documentation: Report whether or not the image currently has EXIF metadata.

methods.name: exif._image.Image.list_all
documentation: List all EXIF tags contained in the image.

documentation:

```
param attribute

image EXIF attribute name

param value

tag value

type value

corresponding Python type
```

```

image file with EXIF metadata

str (file path), bytes (already-read contents), or File

modules.name: exif._app1_metadata
documentation: APP1 metadata interface module for EXIF tags.

classes.name: exif._app1_metadata.App1MetaData

methods.name: exif._app1_metadata.App1MetaData.get_tag_list
documentation: Get a list of EXIF tag attributes present in the image object.

methods.name: exif._app1_metadata.App1MetaData.__getattr__
documentation: If attribute is not a class member, get the value of the EXIF tag of the same name.
APP1 metadata interface class for EXIF tags.
FUTURE: There's quite a few spots where a new Plum Buffer is created for the APP1 body bytes. Consider cleaning this
up to share the same buffer reference. Also, fix the false positive no-member Pylint errors.
